incidents.vector: Compromised private key used to gain infrastructure access and mint uncollateralized stablecoin tokens.
incidents.impact: Attacker created $80 million in fraudulent USR tokens and converted approximately $24.5 million in ETH before the platform was paused.

incidents.vector: Pay2Key ransomware deployed with focus on destruction rather than financial extortion.
incidents.impact: Ransomware deployed against healthcare organization. The group has conducted 170 attacks and collected $8 million in ransom since mid-2025.

incidents.vector: Supply chain attack compromising GitHub Actions to inject credential-stealing malware into official Trivy and KICS vulnerability scanner releases.
incidents.impact: SSH keys, cloud credentials, and Kubernetes tokens stolen from 1,000+ downstream SaaS environments. Attackers also compromised Checkmarx KICS using credentials stolen via Trivy.

incidents.vector: Self-propagating CanisterWorm spreads through exposed Docker APIs, Kubernetes clusters, and Redis servers; wipes data on systems matching Iran timezone or Farsi locale.
incidents.impact: Wiper destroys data on Kubernetes clusters or local machines detected as Iranian. Orchestrated via blockchain-based infrastructure resistant to takedown.

incidents.victim: IoT device owners and DDoS victims worldwide
incidents.vector: Four botnets (Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, Mossad) compromised 3+ million IoT devices including routers and cameras to launch DDoS attacks and extort victims.
incidents.impact: DOJ and international partners dismantled infrastructure. Aisuru alone issued 200,000+ attack commands. Victims reported tens of thousands of dollars in losses.

incidents.vector: Russian national identified vulnerabilities in corporate networks and sold unauthorized access to ransomware groups including Yanluowang.
incidents.impact: Facilitated 52+ ransomware attacks causing $9 million in actual losses. Aleksei Volkov sentenced to 81 months in U.S. federal prison and ordered to pay $9.2 million in restitution.

incidents.victim: Organizations using Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center
incidents.vector: Interlock ransomware gang exploited CVE-2026-20131 (CVSS 10), a zero-day in Cisco FMC allowing unauthenticated remote code execution as root, since January 26.
incidents.impact: Custom RATs deployed, data exfiltration, and ransomware across education, engineering, manufacturing, healthcare, and government sectors. CISA added to KEV catalog.

incidents.vector: Former data analyst contractor stole corporate data during employment, then sent 60+ extortion emails threatening to disclose information to the SEC.
incidents.impact: Company paid $2.5 million in ransom. Cameron Nicholas Curry (27, North Carolina) found guilty; faces up to 12 years in prison.

incidents.vector: Compromised highly privileged identities and abused Microsoft Intune to push remote wipe commands to over 200,000 corporate devices globally.
incidents.impact: Offices across 79 countries shut down. 5,000+ workers sent home in Ireland alone. Devices wiped and defaced with attacker logos. DOJ seized four related domains. Bloomberg reports U.S. government issued guidance to secure Microsoft systems.

incidents.vector: Chinese threat actors exploiting CVE-2026-20963, a deserialization of untrusted data vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoint.
incidents.impact: CISA added the vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Microsoft issued emergency guidance. Active exploitation confirmed in the wild.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack against corporate systems; specific method not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Nucor shut down production at multiple facilities in response to the attack.

incidents.victim: U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC)
incidents.vector: Unauthorized access to email systems of 100+ senior bank regulators sustained for over a year.
incidents.impact: Emails of more than 100 senior officials at the primary U.S. bank regulator were monitored for over 12 months before discovery.

incidents.vector: Details of the breach method not yet disclosed; investigation ongoing.
incidents.impact: Telus confirmed it is investigating a cyber breach. Scope and impact remain under assessment.

incidents.victim: UAE energy and marine services company (unnamed, linked to Saudi Aramco)
incidents.vector: Four waves of targeted spear phishing over six months using industry-specific lures (engineering docs, financial records, travel tickets), deploying custom backdoors including BlackBeard, GhostBackDoor, and Nuso.
incidents.impact: Sustained espionage campaign targeting engineering, finance, and supply chain departments. Provides potential foothold into UAE and Saudi Arabian energy supply chains.

incidents.vector: Custom backdoors (AppleChris, MemFun) deployed via DLL hijacking and WMI lateral movement, targeting domain controllers, web servers, and executive-level assets.
incidents.impact: Strategic intelligence collected on military meeting records, joint exercises with Western armed forces, and C4I command structures. Campaign traced back to 2020 with renewed operations in 2026.

incidents.victim: Thousands of U.S. persons and businesses
incidents.vector: SocksEscort residential proxy service infected routers with malware, selling access to 369,000+ IP addresses to enable fraud and conceal attacker origins.
incidents.impact: DOJ and international partners dismantled infrastructure. Approximately 8,000 infected routers were active. Enabled cryptocurrency account takeovers ($1M), manufacturing fraud ($700K), and military service member fraud ($100K).

incidents.vector: Year-long unauthorized network access beginning November 2024.
incidents.impact: Personal records of 3.4 million individuals exposed. Full scope of the breach only recently confirmed via filing with the Maine Attorney General.

incidents.vector: Phishing and social engineering techniques to take over messaging app accounts, without relying on malware.
incidents.impact: Dutch intelligence agencies warned that Russian state actors are actively targeting users of encrypted messaging apps to compromise their accounts.

incidents.vector: Suspicious cyber activities detected on a sensitive network used to manage wiretaps and intelligence surveillance warrants.
incidents.impact: FBI investigating the seriousness of the incident. The affected network handles legally authorized surveillance operations.
